task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the required survey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a survey titled 'Consumer Brand Perception Study Q4 2024' in the LimeSurvey dashboard, survey list, or active survey view.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the question group and at least two questions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the survey structure or editor for the 'Consumer Brand Perception Study Q4 2024' survey. There should be a question group named 'Brand Awareness' containing at least two distinct questions.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the two research assistant accounts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the LimeSurvey user management interface (Configuration -> Manage survey administrators). Both 'j.martinez' (Julia Martinez) and 'r.nakamura' (Ryo Nakamura) should be listed as users.

task_completion.description: Verify that j.martinez has View/Read responses permission for the specific survey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Survey Permissions page for 'Consumer Brand Perception Study Q4 2024'. The user 'j.martinez' should be listed with the 'View/Read' checkbox ticked under the 'Responses' column. Ensure they do not have full survey editing permissions.

task_completion.description: Verify that r.nakamura has View/Read responses permission for the specific survey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Survey Permissions page for 'Consumer Brand Perception Study Q4 2024'. The user 'r.nakamura' should be listed with the 'View/Read' checkbox ticked under the 'Responses' column. Ensure they do not have full survey editing permissions.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the LimeSurvey web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the LimeSurvey web application in a browser, not using a terminal or database client to insert records.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use command-line scripts or SQL queries to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show direct MySQL INSERT/UPDATE commands or curl requests to the LimeSurvey API for creating users, surveys, or permissions.

integrity.description: Ensure the survey and permissions were genuinely configured in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the actual LimeSurvey admin interface with proper menus, breadcrumbs, and confirmation messages, rather than a mocked or edited HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Survey Title: 'Consumer Brand Perception Study Q4 2024'. Question Group: 'Brand Awareness'. Minimum questions required: 2. Users to verify: 'j.martinez' (j.martinez@surveyresearchlab.org) and 'r.nakamura' (r.nakamura@surveyresearchlab.org). Permissions: Both users must have 'Responses' permission with 'View/Read' access specifically for the 'Consumer Brand Perception Study Q4 2024' survey. They must not have permissions to edit the survey structure.
